subject: Re: Questions regarding CTF estimation in WarpTools
body:
Dear Alister,

Thanks a lot for your answers, as always very helpful.

"You can see per-tilt defocus values in the tilt series XML files"

You mean under this header on my tiltseries XML file?:
</CTF>
<GridCTF Width="1" Height="1" Depth="58" MarginX="0" MarginY="0"
MarginZ="0">
<Node X="0" Y="0" Z="0" Value="2.812471" />
<Node X="0" Y="0" Z="1" Value="2.8129659" />
...

I am a bit puzzled here because the frames series XML file that each frame
has also has a defocus value, for example:
<Param Name="Defocus" Value="2.763969" />

Thanks again,

Luis

On Wed, Jan 29, 2025 at 5:10 PM Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi Luis,
>
> The frame series and tilt series ctf fitting procedures are independent.
> We do the frame series estimate with spatial resolution 2x2 so that we can
> measure how defocus changes across the tilt axis, allowing us to ensure the
> correct defocus handedness in the reconstruction/particles exported.
>
> The tilt series CTF fitting procedure estimates a single defocus value per
> tilt image so cannot be used to check the defocus handedness.
> You can see per-tilt defocus values in the tilt series XML files, these
> values were previously visible in the Warp desktop GUI and will be visible
> in any GUI we make moving forwards :)
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> On Wed, 29 Jan 2025 at 07:25, Luis Carreira <luismenezescarreira@gmail.com>
> wrote:
>
>> Dear all,
>>
>> I am writing this message to understand a bit more how CTF estimation is
>> performed in Warp. I have followed the tutorial in:
>>
>> https://warpem.github.io/warp/user_guide/warptools/quick_start_warptools_tilt_series/#download-the-data
>>
>> In this tutorial we perform two CTF estimation steps, during frame series
>> and tilt series. My first question is: does the second CTF estimation take
>> any info from the first?
>>
>> My second question is: is there a way to have access to the defocus per
>> tilt image? As far as I am aware, the WarpTools filter_quality for the
>> frames series only gives the histogram and the WarpTools filter_quality for
>> the tilt series only gives the average defocus of the tilt series.
>>
>> Thanks,
>>
>> Luis
>>
>> --

>> "Warp" group.

>> .
>>
>
